project.name: cube-cos-api
project.description: Central communication API for CubeCOS cluster nodes — manages firmware, health monitoring, node operations, metrics, and integrations using MDNS peer discovery

nodes.name: Infrastructure Management
nodes.summary: Core cluster infrastructure operations: node lifecycle, firmware upgrades, fixpack patching, and datacenter-level orchestration

crossDomainInteractions: Node lifecycle operations trigger VM evacuation via OpenStack Nova before node reboot or firmware upgrade
Node handler exposes OSD and device management endpoints; node state changes affect Ceph cluster
Infrastructure operations generate system events tracked in InfluxDB and streamed to clients

nodes.name: Compute & Virtualization
nodes.summary: Virtual machine image lifecycle, volume management, instance operations, and GPU resource allocation

crossDomainInteractions: Volume management converts VM images to bootable Cinder volumes backed by Ceph storage

nodes.name: Storage Management
nodes.summary: Ceph OSD operations, block device management, third-party storage backend integration, and volume type configuration

nodes.name: Monitoring & Observability
nodes.summary: Cluster health monitoring, event streaming, time-series metrics collection, notifications, and dashboard integration (Grafana/OpenSearch)

crossDomainInteractions: Health monitoring detects degraded services which may fire alert triggers and generate notifications
Automated health repair may restart services or trigger node-level recovery operations

nodes.name: Security & Access Control
nodes.summary: Authentication via OIDC/SAML, token lifecycle, license verification, and user identity management

crossDomainInteractions: OIDC/SAML middleware validates tokens before allowing access to infrastructure management APIs
License expiration or invalidity contributes to cluster health degradation alerts

nodes.name: Operations & Automation
nodes.summary: Alert trigger management, system tuning parameter control, cluster settings configuration, diagnostic support file generation, and service listing

crossDomainInteractions: System tuning changes are distributed to cluster nodes affecting infrastructure operation parameters

nodes.name: Firmware Upgrade Lifecycle
nodes.summary: End-to-end firmware upgrade process: upload binary, verify integrity, distribute to nodes, execute upgrade, track progress, handle abort

nodes.name: Upload Firmware Binary
nodes.summary: Upload firmware package to API server with multipart form data

nodes.name: Verify MD5 Checksum
nodes.summary: Validate firmware binary integrity using MD5 hash comparison

nodes.name: Distribute to Cluster Nodes
nodes.summary: Push firmware binary to target nodes via SSH for staged upgrade

nodes.name: Execute Firmware Upgrade
nodes.summary: Trigger firmware upgrade via CLI on each target node asynchronously through workqueue

nodes.name: Track Upgrade Progress
nodes.summary: Monitor per-node upgrade status and report progress via SSE streaming

nodes.name: Abort Upgrade
nodes.summary: Cancel in-progress firmware upgrade on specified nodes

nodes.name: Fixpack Patching Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Hotpatch application process: upload fixpack, install on nodes, track progress, rollback if needed

nodes.name: Upload Fixpack
nodes.summary: Upload hotpatch package for cluster-wide application

nodes.name: Install Fixpack
nodes.summary: Apply fixpack via hex_config CLI on target nodes through workqueue

nodes.name: Track Install Progress
nodes.summary: Monitor fixpack installation status across nodes with status lifecycle transitions

nodes.name: Rollback Fixpack
nodes.summary: Reverse fixpack application and restore previous state

nodes.name: Node Lifecycle Operations
nodes.summary: Cluster node management: discovery, listing, drain, reboot, and IPMI power control

nodes.name: Node Discovery & Registry
nodes.summary: Discover cluster nodes via MDNS peer registry and sync state from MongoDB

nodes.name: List Cluster Nodes
nodes.summary: Return inventory of all nodes with roles, status, firmware versions, and device info

nodes.name: Drain Node
nodes.summary: Evacuate VMs from node using OpenStack Nova API before maintenance

nodes.name: Reboot Node
nodes.summary: Execute OS-level reboot on target node

nodes.name: IPMI Power Control
nodes.summary: Remote power management via IPMI for out-of-band node control

nodes.name: VM Image Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Virtual machine image management: list available materials, import with progress tracking, export, and metadata updates

nodes.name: List Image Materials
nodes.summary: Enumerate available image sources and formats for import

nodes.name: Import Image
nodes.summary: Import VM image via hex_sdk with pseudo-terminal progress tracking, processed by background operator

nodes.name: Track Import Progress
nodes.summary: Monitor image import progress stored in MongoDB and stream via SSE

nodes.name: Export Image
nodes.summary: Export existing VM image from Glance for download or backup

nodes.name: Update Image Metadata
nodes.summary: Modify image properties and metadata in Glance

nodes.name: Volume Management
nodes.summary: Cinder volume operations: listing, export, and image-to-volume conversion

nodes.name: List Volumes
nodes.summary: Query and return all Cinder volumes with status and metadata

nodes.name: Export Volume
nodes.summary: Export volume data for backup or migration

nodes.name: Image-to-Volume Conversion
nodes.summary: Convert a VM image into a bootable Cinder volume asynchronously via workqueue

nodes.name: Ceph OSD Operations
nodes.summary: Ceph storage device management: add disks, promote/demote between tiers, remove, and restart OSDs

nodes.name: Add OSD Disk
nodes.summary: Add new block device as Ceph OSD via hex_sdk

nodes.name: Promote OSD Tier
nodes.summary: Promote OSD to higher performance storage tier

nodes.name: Demote OSD Tier
nodes.summary: Demote OSD to lower performance storage tier

nodes.name: Remove OSD
nodes.summary: Safely remove OSD from Ceph cluster with data rebalancing

nodes.name: Storage Backend Integration
nodes.summary: Third-party storage integration: configure Cinder backends, manage vendor/model definitions, verify connectivity

nodes.name: List Vendors & Models
nodes.summary: Enumerate supported storage vendors and hardware models

nodes.name: Create Cinder Backend
nodes.summary: Configure new Cinder storage backend for third-party array

nodes.name: Verify Backend Connectivity
nodes.summary: Test connection and authentication to configured storage backend

nodes.name: Update Backend Configuration
nodes.summary: Modify existing storage backend parameters

nodes.name: Health Monitoring & Repair
nodes.summary: Cluster health assessment: collect health data from InfluxDB, evaluate per-service status, trigger repairs, stream updates via SSE

nodes.name: Health Summary
nodes.summary: Aggregate overall cluster health status across all services and modules

nodes.name: Per-Service Health Check
nodes.summary: Evaluate individual service/module health with status history from InfluxDB

nodes.name: Periodic Health Resync
nodes.summary: Background operator periodically re-syncs health history data

nodes.name: Trigger Health Repair
nodes.summary: Execute automated repair operation for degraded services

nodes.name: Stream Health Updates
nodes.summary: Push real-time health status changes to clients via Server-Sent Events

nodes.name: Event Tracking & Streaming
nodes.summary: System event management: query events from InfluxDB, filter by type/host/time, stream new events via SSE

nodes.name: Query Events
nodes.summary: Retrieve system/host/instance events from InfluxDB using Flux queries

nodes.name: Filter & Paginate Events
nodes.summary: Apply filters (type, rank, host, time range) and pagination to event results

nodes.name: Stream Live Events
nodes.summary: Push new events to clients in real-time via SSE connection

nodes.name: Metrics Collection & Dashboard
nodes.summary: Time-series metrics: periodic summary refresh, detailed per-resource metrics, Grafana deep-links, SSE streaming

nodes.name: Periodic Metrics Refresh
nodes.summary: Background operator refreshes cached metric summary every 60 seconds

nodes.name: Datacenter Metrics Summary
nodes.summary: Return aggregated CPU, memory, disk, network usage for datacenter

nodes.name: Detailed Resource Metrics
nodes.summary: Query InfluxDB for per-host/VM time-series metrics with rankings

nodes.name: Generate Grafana Dashboard Link
nodes.summary: Construct deep-link to Grafana dashboard for specific host/instance/network/storage

nodes.name: Authentication & Token Lifecycle
nodes.summary: User authentication: credential validation via Keycloak, access/refresh token creation, session management, logout

nodes.name: Submit Credentials
nodes.summary: User submits username/password for authentication

nodes.name: Keycloak OIDC Validation
nodes.summary: Validate credentials against Keycloak and obtain OIDC tokens

nodes.name: Issue Access & Refresh Tokens
nodes.summary: Return access token and refresh token pair to client

nodes.name: Request Auth Middleware
nodes.summary: OIDC/SAML middleware validates tokens on every API request

nodes.name: Logout & Session Cleanup
nodes.summary: Terminate session and redirect via SAML logout flow

nodes.name: License Management
nodes.summary: Cluster/host license lifecycle: listing, verification, import, and auto-sync via file watcher

nodes.name: List Licenses
nodes.summary: Return all cluster and host licenses with product/expiry info

nodes.name: Verify License
nodes.summary: Validate license file authenticity and check expiration

nodes.name: Import License
nodes.summary: Import and activate new cluster or host license via CLI

nodes.name: Auto-Sync License State
nodes.summary: Background operator watches license files and synchronizes state on changes

nodes.name: Alert Trigger Management
nodes.summary: Automation trigger lifecycle: CRUD operations, script verification via dry-run, enable/disable control

nodes.name: List Triggers
nodes.summary: Return all configured alert triggers with status

nodes.name: Create Trigger
nodes.summary: Define new alert trigger with conditions and actions via hex_sdk

nodes.name: Dry-Run Script Verification
nodes.summary: Validate trigger script execution without side effects

nodes.name: Enable/Disable Trigger
nodes.summary: Toggle trigger active state and sync policy file

nodes.name: System Tuning Control
nodes.summary: Cluster tuning parameters: list specifications, update values, distribute across nodes, reset to defaults

nodes.name: List Tuning Specifications
nodes.summary: Enumerate available tuning parameters with current values and defaults

nodes.name: Update Tuning Parameter
nodes.summary: Modify tuning parameter value in policy YAML and queue distribution

nodes.name: Distribute to Cluster
nodes.summary: Push tuning changes to all cluster nodes for consistent configuration

nodes.name: Reset to Defaults
nodes.summary: Restore tuning parameters to factory default values

nodes.name: Diagnostic Support Bundle
nodes.summary: Generate, manage, and download diagnostic bundles for troubleshooting cluster issues

nodes.name: Create Support Bundle
nodes.summary: Trigger diagnostic file creation collecting logs, configs, and state from cluster nodes

nodes.name: List Support Files
nodes.summary: Enumerate available diagnostic bundles with creation time and size

nodes.name: Download Support File
nodes.summary: Download diagnostic bundle from S3 storage

nodes.name: Delete Support File
nodes.summary: Remove diagnostic bundle from storage to free space

edges.description: Node lifecycle operations trigger VM evacuation via OpenStack Nova before node reboot or firmware upgrade
Node handler exposes OSD and device management endpoints; node state changes affect Ceph cluster
Infrastructure operations generate system events tracked in InfluxDB and streamed to clients
Volume management converts VM images to bootable Cinder volumes backed by Ceph storage
Health monitoring detects degraded services which may fire alert triggers and generate notifications
OIDC/SAML middleware validates tokens before allowing access to infrastructure management APIs
License expiration or invalidity contributes to cluster health degradation alerts
System tuning changes are distributed to cluster nodes affecting infrastructure operation parameters
Automated health repair may restart services or trigger node-level recovery operations
